title: centaurea erivanensis subsp. holargyrea
intro: Centaurea erivanensis subsp. holargyrea, also known as Erivan cornflower or Armenian cornflower, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Armenia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, and is also used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Traditionally, Centaurea erivanensis subsp. holargyrea is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
